prompt: Build something like Notion for doctors.
original_prompt: Build something like Notion for doctors.

assumptions: The application is for medical professionals
Notion is used as a reference for the type of application
clarifications_received: A secure workspace for medical notes, managing patient records, and scheduling appointments. Strict HIPAA compliance and data sensitivity are absolutely required.
Doctors can write clinical notes and view patient records. Nurses can view records and manage appointments.
No, this is for internal clinic use only.
questions: What specific features of Notion would you like to replicate for doctors?
What type of medical professionals will be using this application (e.g. general practitioners, specialists, nurses)?
Are there any specific medical domains or specialties that this application should focus on (e.g. patient management, medical research, telemedicine)?

entities.name: Patient
entities.description: Patients whose records are managed by the application

relations.description: A doctor can write multiple clinical notes
A nurse can manage multiple appointments
A patient can have multiple clinical notes
A patient can have multiple appointments

business_rules.name: HIPAA Compliance
business_rules.description: All patient data must be encrypted and access-controlled

business_rules.name: Role-Based Access Control
business_rules.description: Doctors can create and view clinical notes, nurses can view patient records and manage appointments

data_flows.name: User Authentication
data_flows.description: User credentials are verified for authentication

data_flows.name: Patient Data Retrieval
data_flows.description: Patient data is retrieved for display

data_flows.name: Clinical Note Creation
data_flows.description: Clinical notes are created and associated with patients

data_flows.name: Appointment Scheduling
data_flows.description: Appointments are scheduled and associated with patients

endpoints.description: Get a list of clinical notes
Get a clinical note by id

errors.description: The table User_Appointment has a foreign key to the User table, but the User table does not have a corresponding foreign key to the User_Appointment table.
The table User_ClinicalNote has a foreign key to the ClinicalNote table, but the ClinicalNote table does not have a corresponding foreign key to the User_ClinicalNote table.
The endpoint /users/{id} does not have a validation rule to check if the user exists before updating or deleting.
The endpoint /patients/{id} does not have a validation rule to check if the patient exists before updating or deleting.
The endpoint /clinical-notes/{id} does not have a validation rule to check if the clinical note exists before updating or deleting.
The endpoint /appointments/{id} does not have a validation rule to check if the appointment exists before updating or deleting.
The form on the page /patients/create does not have a validation rule to check if the patient's date of birth is valid.
The form on the page /clinical-notes/create does not have a validation rule to check if the clinical note's patient id is valid.
The form on the page /appointments/create does not have a validation rule to check if the appointment's patient id and doctor id are valid.
The permissions matrix does not define the permissions for the Nurse role to view clinical notes and appointments.
The premium plan gates do not define the features and restrictions for the Basic and Premium plans.

warnings.description: The table User has a column soft_deleted_at, but it is not used in any of the endpoints or forms.
The table Patient has a column soft_deleted_at, but it is not used in any of the endpoints or forms.
The table ClinicalNote has a column soft_deleted_at, but it is not used in any of the endpoints or forms.
The table Appointment has a column soft_deleted_at, but it is not used in any of the endpoints or forms.

conflicts.description: The endpoint /users/{id} has a conflict with the endpoint /patients/{id} because they both update the user's information.
conflicts.resolution_strategy: Merge the two endpoints into one endpoint that updates both the user's and patient's information.

conflicts.description: The form on the page /patients/create has a conflict with the form on the page /clinical-notes/create because they both create a new patient.
conflicts.resolution_strategy: Remove the form on the page /clinical-notes/create and use the form on the page /patients/create to create a new patient.

repairs.error_description: The table User_Appointment has a foreign key to the User table, but the User table does not have a corresponding foreign key to the User_Appointment table.
The table User_ClinicalNote has a foreign key to the ClinicalNote table, but the ClinicalNote table does not have a corresponding foreign key to the User_ClinicalNote table.

The form on the page /clinical-notes/create does not have a validation rule to check if the clinical note's patient id is valid.
The form on the page /appointments/create does not have a validation rule to check if the appointment's patient id and doctor id are valid.
The permissions matrix does not define the permissions for the Nurse role to view clinical notes and appointments.

blocking_issues: The permissions matrix does not define the permissions for the Nurse role to view clinical notes and appointments.
The premium plan gates do not define the features and restrictions for the Basic and Premium plans.

mermaid_diagrams.api_sequence:
sequenceDiagram
    participant Client as Client
    participant API as API
    participant Database as Database
    Client->>API: Request to create patient
    API->>Database: Insert patient data
    Database-->>API: Patient data inserted
    API-->>Client: Patient created successfully
    Client->>API: Request to read patient data
    API->>Database: Retrieve patient data
    Database-->>API: Patient data retrieved
    API-->>Client: Patient data sent
